reasoning: There are just roughly two months left before the January 1, 2026 deadline. The current administration has actively prioritized deregulating AI (e.g., President Trump repealing Biden's AI Executive Order 14110 and replacing it with one aimed at removing barriers). Major comprehensive AI regulatory proposals have either stalled or had relevant provisions stripped from them (as seen with the 10-year state moratorium provision stripped from H.R. 1). Smaller targeted bills addressing deepfakes or AI-generated content have advanced, but the question criteria explicitly exclude such bills (like the TAKE IT DOWN Act) as they target the publication of content rather than substantively regulating the development or models of AI systems themselves. Congress does not typically advance and pass comprehensive structural tech regulation within a span of a few weeks at the end of a year, particularly in an environment favoring deregulation. The crowd forecast is at 1%, and the evidence directly aligning with this expectation makes a "Yes" resolution highly unlikely by the deadline. I am therefore selecting the minimum allowable estimate of 2%.
